summary:
Tafenoquine primarily serves as a radical cure for Plasmodium vivax infections by eliminating liver-stage parasites and preventing recurrence; it offers an extended duration effect compared to standard antimalarials but has limited application outside this specific context given potential side effects and restrictions.
To address another aspect mentioned earlier regarding alternative options not directly related here:

Option B about treating malaria symptoms does have merit since many anti-malarial drugs can alleviate acute illness caused by various species including falciparum which isn't specifically targeted by tafenoquine’s mechanism yet still falls under general treatment scope rather than being exclusive feature distinguishing one drug from others within class effectively ruling out broad symptom relief claim solely based upon uniqueness factor highlighted above concerning prevention/recurrence control via elimination strategy employed uniquely well suited towards P.vivax lifecycle characteristics making primary answer more accurate despite overlapping therapeutic benefits shared among multiple agents used clinically today across different stages/types of infection processes managed through combination therapy approaches when necessary depending patient needs severity etc...
